enonce.texte:
Read the text below about social media and answer the questions that follow.

Text: What is Social Media?

Social media are websites and apps that allow people to communicate and share content. Examples include Facebook, Instagram, TikTok, and WhatsApp. People use social media to stay in touch with friends and family, share photos and videos, and learn about news. However, social media can also have negative effects. Some people spend too much time on it. Others face cyberbullying or see fake news. It is important to use social media wisely and be kind to others online.
images: An illustration showing various social media logos: Facebook, Instagram, TikTok, and WhatsApp.

questions.texte: Identify the social media platforms mentioned in the text.
questions.reponse: The social media platforms mentioned are: Facebook, Instagram, TikTok, and WhatsApp.

questions.texte: Explain why social media can be negative according to the text.
questions.reponse: According to the text, social media can be negative because some people spend too much time on it, others face cyberbullying, and some see fake news.

questions.texte: Analyse the author's advice about using social media.
questions.reponse: The author advises that it is important to use social media wisely and to be kind to others online. This means being careful about how much time we spend on social media and treating others with respect.

intitule: Grammar Practice: Verbs Expressing Likes and Dislikes

enonce.texte: Complete the sentences with the correct verb expressing likes or dislikes: love, enjoy, like, hate, can't stand, dislike.

questions.texte: I _______ sharing photos on Instagram. It's fun!
She _______ spending too much time on social media. It's a waste of time.
We _______ chatting with friends on WhatsApp every evening.
He _______ seeing negative comments on his posts.
My parents _______ using social media. They prefer talking on the phone.
I _______ watching funny videos on TikTok. They make me laugh!

intitule: Writing a Short Message About Social Media

enonce.texte: You want to tell your friend about your experience with social media. Write a short message (4-5 sentences) expressing what you like or dislike about social media. Use at least two verbs expressing likes or dislikes.

questions.texte: Write your message to your friend.
questions.reponse: Example answer: "Hi friend! I love using Instagram to share photos of my daily life. However, I hate seeing negative comments on people's posts. I also enjoy watching funny videos on TikTok because they make me laugh. What about you? Do you like social media?"

enonce.texte:
Read the situation below and write a short dialogue between two friends discussing how to cope with social media problems.

Situation: Your friend spends too much time on social media and feels stressed. You want to help them find a balance. Write a dialogue where you give advice to your friend.
images: A teenager looking at a phone with a worried expression, showing the stress of social media use.

questions.texte: Write a dialogue (at least 6 exchanges) between you and your friend.
questions.reponse: Example dialogue: You: Hey, you look stressed. What's wrong? Friend: I've been on social media all day. I feel so tired and anxious. You: I understand. Social media can be overwhelming sometimes. Friend: I know, but I can't stop checking my phone. You: Maybe you should set a time limit. You can use your phone for only 30 minutes in the evening. Friend: That's a good idea. What else can I do? You: You can also turn off notifications. That helps me focus on other things. Friend: Thanks for the advice. I will try that.

intitule: Reading Comprehension: A Tweet About Social Media

enonce.texte:
Read the tweet below and answer the questions.

Tweet: @CameroonTeen

"Social media is great for connecting with friends, but it can be addictive. I love watching videos on TikTok, but I don't like how much time I spend on it. I'm going to limit my screen time to 1 hour per day. #DigitalWellness #SocialMediaBalance"
images: A smartphone screen showing a tweet with the text and hashtags #DigitalWellness and #SocialMediaBalance.

questions.texte: Identify what the tweeter loves and dislikes about social media.
questions.reponse: The tweeter loves watching videos on TikTok but dislikes how much time they spend on it.

questions.texte: Explain the action the tweeter plans to take.
questions.reponse: The tweeter plans to limit their screen time to 1 hour per day to have a better balance with social media.

questions.texte: Analyse the hashtags used by the tweeter and explain their meaning.
questions.reponse: #DigitalWellness means taking care of one's mental and physical health when using digital devices. #SocialMediaBalance means finding a healthy balance between using social media and other activities in life.

enonce.texte:
You are a journalist for your school newspaper. Write a short article about the problems students face with social media. Include:

  • A title
  • At least three problems mentioned (e.g., addiction, cyberbullying, fake news)
  • At least two verbs expressing likes or dislikes
  • Advice on how to cope with these problems
images: An illustration showing a student surrounded by social media icons, looking overwhelmed.

questions.reponse: Example answer: Title: Social Media: A Double-Edged Sword Social media is a big part of our lives, but it comes with problems. Many students love using social media, but they hate the negative effects it can have. First, addiction is a serious issue. Students spend hours scrolling through feeds instead of studying. Second, cyberbullying is common. Many students dislike receiving mean comments online. Third, fake news spreads easily. Students often don't know what information to trust. To cope with these problems, students should limit their screen time, ignore negative comments, and verify information before sharing. Let's use social media wisely!
